Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am 27 years old, born in New Jersey and grew up in New York City. Growing up I spent most of my time outside. It was a way to escape the reality of the world. I liked riding bikes, hiking, and skating. I always wanted to help the community and I wanted to make a difference in the world. I am a political junkie and my friends will tell you I know a lot about politics. I always wanted to work in government. I had my sights set on going to college and work in politics. I applied to school to study political science and I ended up getting my degree in sociology. I was not sure what I wanted to do but I knew that I wanted to get involved in politics. I started working for a member of congress in New York. I then worked on a campaign and it was not until I was working on the campaign that I found out that there is a possibility to make money doing this. I decided that I wanted to go to law school and I applied to law school. I decided to go to a school in South Carolina because I thought that it would be a good experience. It was an interesting experience. I got a degree in political science and then I went to work for a politician in South Carolina. I decided to take a break from politics and work for a large law firm in New York City. It was a good experience and I got a lot of knowledge about the law. I decided that I wanted to go to law school and I applied to a few schools. I ended up going to Columbia Law School in New York City. I got a law degree and then I went to work for a large law firm in New York City. It was a good experience and I got a lot of knowledge about the law. I decided that I wanted to go to law school and I applied to a few schools. I ended up going to Columbia Law School in New York City. I got a law degree and then I went to work for a large law firm in New York City. It was a good experience and I got a lot of knowledge about the law.

{'text': "Before this study you answered an interview question.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am a 23 year old female who grew up in a small suburb in the west valley of Phoenix. Because of this, I never wanted to be a small town girl. As soon as I turned 18, I had the opportunity to move to California for 6 months and I have never looked back. I love new experiences and have been privileged enough to visit 32 countries. And as the US citizen, that has been the most valuable lessons that I have learned while abroad. Moving from country to country can be difficult at times but will never stop me from making a move when opportunity knocks.

After returning to the states, I traveled to New York, Las Vegas, Washington D.C, New Orleans, Los Angeles, and San Diego as a tour guide on on a 3 month road trip. I was able to complete my undergraduate work with a double major in Journalism and English with a focus in Professional Writing. Over the past few years, I have had the opportunity to hone in my skills and help numerous people. From teaching history to high school students to one on one mentoring to younger females, I have loved sharing my life experience with them.

As I travel and work towards being an English language teacher, I am hoping to prepare them for life in our ever changing and sophisticated global society. Furthermore, I have aided both graduate and undergraduate students with their essays. I have tutored children aged 7-17 in English Language Arts. And I also have a background in proofreading for numerous businesses in England.

{'text': "Before this study you answered an interview question.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.

Answer: I am 25 years old and grew up in ____. I would say the biggest thing to shape who I am is my upbringing. My mom was pretty hard on me growing up but not as hard as she could have been. I was kind of a really anxious kid and she would consistently shout at me about wanting to beat me into submission. I never told her I was on meds because I was afraid she would stop them, and the thought of it just breaks my heart. I still struggle with a lot of anxiety as well. It would be so awesome to go back and be a kid again and make decisions that would allow me to get the education and work I want. As for people I hold in my heart, I don’t really have many but I do remember fondly the girl who was just getting into it with me when I met (2nd person on the team). Despite our vastly different perspectives, we were able to connect on a few things and actually come together over a project we’re starting with her husband and a few other people. I just wanted to give a little background on myself, answer what you wanted to know. I’d say I’m most proud of my ability to stick with things I start. I kind of quit a lot of stuff that I start, whether it be learning how to run or a university, but I don’t think I’m a quitter. There’s just only so much time in life and sometimes you just have to decide between a million different things you want to do and stop worrying about it.

{'text': "Before this study you answered an interview question.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am 73 years old, married to a wonderful lady named Rosalie since 1981. I worked for 35 years as an educator, starting as a teacher in LAUSD then moving to what was then called the University of Southern California School of Education. I was there until 1991, then moved to National University where I worked 13 years until retiring in 2004. I currently teach part time at CSUF and write a blog every week. Rosalie and I love to travel, go to movies, read the newspaper, enjoy our two grandchildren, watch CNN, eat organic food, and try to fix up a vintage house in Fullerton. The house has become a labor of love. We have a dog and 6 chickens in the back yard, fresh organic eggs every day. We have lived in the same house in Fullerton for 40 years. I have four sons and two daughter-in-laws, six grandchildren, and one great-granddaughter on the way! I have spent the past 10 years as a baseball dad, volunteering as a team parent for our boys' baseball teams. We’ve traveled all over the country for tournaments. My youngest boy has just signed a scholarship with Dixie State University in St. George, Utah.
